name: Supabase
one_liner: Open-source Firebase alternative providing a hosted Postgres database with auto-generated REST and GraphQL APIs, auth, storage, edge functions, and a project Management API.
access_notes: Free tier gives an instantly usable project with working API credentials (anon public key, service_role secret key, and JWT-based auth) with no payment or human approval. The Management API uses a personal access token generated from the dashboard.
api_notes: Each project auto-generates a REST API via PostgREST (full CRUD over every table/view plus stored-procedure RPC) and a GraphQL endpoint via pg_graphql. Separate services expose Auth (GoTrue), Storage, Realtime, and Edge Functions. A project/org Management API (management API at api.supabase.com) covers creating projects, branches, secrets, and config. Official client SDKs (supabase-js, Python, etc.) wrap all of these. Combined surface is well over 100 operations.
mcp_notes: Supabase ships an official MCP server at github.com/supabase/mcp (documented at supabase.com/docs/guides/ai-tools/mcp) for managing tables, fetching config, and querying data via AI assistants. Many community MCP servers also exist.
main_blocker: None significant; free self-serve credentials and public REST/GraphQL APIs. Data-plane auth is per-project (project URL + keys) rather than a single global API, so an integration must handle per-project credentials.

evidence.claim: Supabase auto-generates a REST API from the database via PostgREST, designed to build without writing code.
Supabase ships an official MCP server connecting AI tools directly to Supabase for managing tables, fetching config, and querying data.
Official MCP server documentation is hosted by the vendor.

open_questions: Exact operation count of the Management API vs data-plane API; whether GraphQL (pg_graphql) is enabled by default on all plans.
